step_scores.reason: Core definition of algorithmic trading; foundational for all subsequent lessons. High importance and course relevance. Exam signal is weak (no text), but this is a fundamental concept likely to be tested.

Market trends and comparison of trading approaches provide context and motivation. Relevant to CLO5 (current trends). Not as critical as step1 for core skills, but valuable for understanding the landscape.
Trading mechanism, order book, order types, and slippage are essential operational knowledge for any algorithmic trader. Directly supports CLO1 and CLO4. High importance and course relevance.
Long/short positions, PnL calculation, and mark-to-market are fundamental quantitative skills. Directly supports CLO2 and CLO3. High importance and course relevance.
Leverage, margin, buying power, and trading systems are critical for risk management and strategy deployment. Supports CLO3 and CLO4. High importance and course relevance.
Infrastructure considerations (data, hardware, connectivity) are important for practical implementation. Supports CLO4 and CLO5. Slightly less central than core trading concepts, but still relevant.
